data.body:
## Description

**What**: Enhance the Standard tier sonnet code reviewer (code-reviewer-standard.md) with researched best-practice sub-criteria across all 5 scoring dimensions, incorporating project-specific patterns and reducing generic false positives.

**Why**: The Standard tier handles ~30-40% of reviews and performs comprehensive 5-dimension analysis. Its current checklist items are generic — they don't account for this project's conventions (bash hook scripts, Python ticket scripts, markdown skill files) or suppress findings already caught by pre-commit hooks (ruff, mypy).

**Scope**:
- IN: Updating all 5 dimension checklists in code-reviewer-standard.md with context-aware sub-criteria; adding file-type routing (bash vs Python vs markdown); researching current best practices for each dimension; adding project-pattern exclusions
- OUT: Changing scoring thresholds; modifying the output contract; adding new dimensions beyond the existing 5

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the Standard reviewer checklist contains file-type-aware sub-criteria that apply different checks for bash scripts (hooks/scripts), Python code (ticket scripts), and markdown files (skills/docs)
  ← Satisfies: 'Review Intelligence & Precision'
- When this story is complete, each dimension's checklist items include at least one project-specific pattern check (e.g., 'bash scripts must quote variables in conditionals', 'Python scripts must use subprocess instead of os.system')
  ← Satisfies: 'Review Intelligence & Precision'
- When this story is complete, unit tests verify the updated agent file structure and checklist completeness
  ← Satisfies: 'Review Intelligence & Precision'

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Delta file is composed with reviewer-base.md — changes must not duplicate base guidance
- [Performance] Standard tier uses sonnet — checklist length affects token usage and review time
- [Testing] Validate against sample diffs from recent commits to verify reduced false positive rate
